input.background: The experiment involves testing a hypothesis about the proportion of defective items in a population. The data sequence is gbggbgbgbggbgbg, where b denotes defective items and g denotes good items. The null hypothesis is H0: p = p0 = 0.10, and the alternative hypothesis is H1: p = p1 = 0.20. The goal is to reject H0 with a probability of 0.05 when it is true and accept H0 with a probability of 0.20 when H1 is true. Using a sequential testing plan, the task is to determine whether to accept or reject the lot based on the observations provided.
input.data:
$$
\mathrm{s}_{\mathrm{b}} n+b_1<d_n<\mathrm{s}_{\mathrm{b}} n+b_0
$$
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline $n$ & $x_n$ & $d_n$ & Acceptance \# & Rejection \# \\
\hline 1 & 0 & 0 & - & - \\
\hline 2 & 1 & 1 & - & - \\
\hline 3 & 0 & 1 & - & - \\
\hline 4 & 0 & 1 & - & - \\
\hline 5 & 1 & 2 & - & 5 \\
\hline 6 & 0 & 2 & - & 5 \\
\hline 7 & 1 & 3 & - & 5 \\

\hline 15 & 0 & 6 & 0 & 6 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
input.question: Establish the acceptance and rejection lines for the sequential testing plan.

output.answer: The acceptance line is given by: $0.1452n - 1.9214$. The rejection line is given by: $0.1452n + 3.4190$.
